sop.title: Debug Screenpipe E2E test failure in Cursor IDE

steps.description: User opened the openclaw-video-skill-pipeline project in Cursor IDE, with SKILL.md file active in the editor

steps.action: Run E2E test command in terminal
steps.description: User attempted to run npm run e2e:screenpipe in the integrated PowerShell terminal. The test failed because curl could not connect to Screenpipe API at port 3030

steps.action: Attempt to set environment variable (PowerShell syntax error)
steps.description: User tried to set SCREENPIPE_URL using bash syntax in PowerShell, resulting in CommandNotFoundException

steps.action: Attempt to run screenpipe command (not found)
steps.description: User attempted to run screenpipe CLI which is not installed or in PATH on Windows

An AI (Claude) provided a detailed explanation of the issues: Screenpipe not running, incorrect PowerShell syntax, and missing CLI. It also provided corrected steps to install Screenpipe, verify the port, and run the test properly

steps.action: Compose prompt for AI assistant to run E2E test
steps.description: User created a detailed copy-paste prompt for Claude with the full E2E test procedure including prerequisites (Screenpipe running, API keys in .env), and step-by-step instructions to run the pipeline

sop.notes: The timeline shows a debugging session where the user attempted to run an end-to-end test for the openclaw-video-skill-pipeline but encountered failures because Screenpipe was not running. The terminal shows the actual command execution and error messages, followed by an AI response explaining the issues and providing troubleshooting guidance. User was composing a prompt to delegate the fix to an AI assistant.
